name: Timsbury
coordsSource: web-verified:streetmap(New Road Timsbury grid 434679/125636);wikipedia(A3057 N of Romsey); cross-checked listing-centroid (n=10, ~70m); 2026-06-04
overview: Timsbury is a small village in the civil parish of Michelmersh and Timsbury in the Test Valley district of Hampshire, lying along the A3057 about 3 miles north of Romsey, towards Stockbridge. It has a population of roughly 400 and shares a boundary with the neighbouring village of Michelmersh. The village grew from a traditional settlement centred on the Manor House and the Grade II listed Church of St Andrew, and sits on the eastern side of the River Test valley. Day-to-day amenities are limited, with the nearest full facilities in Romsey, but the village has a recreation ground and an active community football club.
character: A quiet, linear Test Valley village of period cottages, farmhouses and newer homes along the A3057, surrounded by farmland and close to the River Test. The feel is rural and community-minded, with a recreation ground, playing fields and the long-running Michelmersh & Timsbury football club at its heart.
amenities: Timsbury Recreation Ground and sports pavilion (Mannyngham Way)
Church of St Andrew (Grade II listed)
Neighbouring Michelmersh village with St Mary's Church and Jubilee Hall
Full shopping, supermarkets and services in Romsey (~3 mi)
Mottisfont & Dunbridge station and the A3057 for wider travel

commutes.notes: Nearest town for shops and services, via the A3057
From Mottisfont & Dunbridge (~2.5 mi) on the Wessex Main Line, or faster from Winchester

prices.summary: Timsbury is a sought-after Test Valley village; housing is mostly detached and period property with a range of cottages, and prices generally sit in the £450k-£900k+ bracket, with larger houses well above. Supply is limited and demand strong given the Romsey proximity.

thingsToDo: Walking the Monarch's Way and Test Valley footpaths
Fishing and river walks on the River Test
Michelmersh & Timsbury sports and community events
placesToEat: Pubs in Michelmersh and nearby villages
Wide choice of pubs, cafes and restaurants in Romsey (~3 mi)
The Mill Arms and other Test Valley pubs within a short drive
pros: Quiet Test Valley village close to Romsey
Strong community with recreation ground and sports club
Good local primary schools and reach of The Romsey School
Excellent countryside, river and Mottisfont nearby
cons: Limited amenities in the village itself
Nearest fast mainline services require a drive to Winchester
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a friendly, well-connected Test Valley village within a few minutes of Romsey, with good schools and countryside on the doorstep.

sources.label: Test Valley BC - Michelmersh & Timsbury Parish Profile 2024
